title: Student loan repayment
description: Practice understanding of repayment vocabulary and repayment terms and conditions.

assessment_items.question_content:
Under the standard repayment plan for federal student loans your loan will be split into equal monthly payments.

**How long does it take to pay back your student loans under the standard plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The standard repayment plan for federal student loans is usually set up as a $10$-year term.

**What is the payment style for the standard repayment plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: payments go up every two years
based on how much you earn

assessment_items.question_content:
Unless you elect differently, all federal student loans automatically go into a standard repayment plan.

**Which statement is true about the standard repayment plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You pay more interest than with income-driven plans.
Monthly payments are lower than graduated plans.
You pay less interest than with extended plans.
The plan automatically extends to $25$ years.

assessment_items.question_content:
If you are struggling to make your student loan payments, one of the options you may have is to switch to an income-driven repayment plan.

**Which of the following best describes income-driven repayment plans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Payments are a fixed amount for $10$ years.
Payments adjust based on the borrower's income and family size.
Payments start low but increase automatically every two years.
Payments cover only the interest accruing on the loan.

assessment_items.question_content:
The goal of an income-driven repayment plan is to make student loan payments more affordable until your income increases through a better-paying job or a raise.

**What happens if your income does not significantly increase while on this plan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You have to pay the loan off in full with additional penalties.
Your loan balance may be forgiven after $20$ or $25$ years of qualifying payments.
You automatically switch to a standard repayment plan.
Your payments remain the same for the entire term, regardless of income changes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The goal of an income-driven repayment plan is to make student loan payments more affordable until your income increases through a better-paying job or a raise.

**What happens if your income increases significantly?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You might be switched to a standard repayment plan.
The interest rate on your loan goes down.

assessment_items.question_content:
Certain repayment plans have provisions that allow for your federal student loan to be forgiven.

**Which federal student loan repayment plan includes the potential for loan forgiveness after years of qualifying payments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Sometimes the career you choose can qualify you for student loan forgiveness, regardless of the loan repayment plan.

**Which job might make you eligible for student loan forgiveness?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: public service employee, such as a government or non-profit worker
research scientist at a private tech company

assessment_items.question_content:
Sometimes the career you choose can qualify you for student loan forgiveness, regardless of the loan repayment plan.

**Which job might make you eligible for student loan forgiveness?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: lawyer in a corporate law firm
marketing executive in a private company

assessment_items.question_content:
Student loan repayment plans come with both benefits and drawbacks. One of the benefits is being able to pay your student loan off in the shortest amount of time.

**Which of the repayment plans has that benefit ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Student loan repayment plans each offer unique benefits and drawbacks. One of the benefits is the ability to adjust your monthly payments to a more affordable level.

**Which of the repayment plans offer this benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Student loan repayment plans come with both benefits and drawbacks. One benefit is choosing a repayment plan that lets you have a more affordable monthly payment.

**Which drawback is associated with this benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: difficulty in qualifying for future financial aid
more interest paid over the life of the loan
